channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: FIFA hydration breaks have sparked criticism. But what do they actually do?
item.description: For the first time in World Cup history, FIFA is mandating all soccer players take hydration breaks to protect them from the threats of extreme heat. But the new rule has sparked criticism.
item.content:encoded:
For the first time in World Cup history, FIFA is mandating all soccer players take hydration breaks to protect them from the threats of extreme heat. But the new rule has sparked criticism.

(Image credit: Julio Cortez)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Over 1,000 people killed during Gaza ceasefire, Palestinian authorities say
item.description: Israeli operations in the Gaza Strip have killed 1,005 Palestinians since a ceasefire was reached between Israel and the militant group Hamas last October, according to Gaza Health Ministry.
item.content:encoded:
Israeli operations in the Gaza Strip have killed 1,005 Palestinians since a ceasefire was reached between Israel and the militant group Hamas last October, according to Gaza Health Ministry.

(Image credit: Majdi Mohammed)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Here's how much the Iran war cost -- and how its effects will linger
item.description: More than three months after the U.S. and Israel launched their war on Iran, the costs and aftereffects are felt around the world.
item.content:encoded:
More than three months after the U.S. and Israel launched their war on Iran, the costs and aftereffects are felt around the world.

(Image credit: Firdous Nazir)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Uzbekistan's first World Cup appearance is a dream come true for players and fans
item.description: The World Cup debut of Uzbekistan signals the region's growing confidence on the global stage and, some argue, the arrival of a "golden generation" making its mark in sports, culture and science.
item.content:encoded: The World Cup debut of Uzbekistan signals the region's growing confidence on the global stage and, some argue, the arrival of a "golden generation" making its mark in sports, culture and science.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: An inside look at President Trump's campaign to acquire Greenland
item.description: New Yorker writer Ben Taub says while the idea of acquiring Greenland is out of the headlines, it hasn't been dropped. Taub describes how Trump's ongoing efforts have broken the trust of our allies.
item.content:encoded: New Yorker writer Ben Taub says while the idea of acquiring Greenland is out of the headlines, it hasn't been dropped. Taub describes how Trump's ongoing efforts have broken the trust of our allies.
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: Greetings from Maputo, Mozambique's capital, shaped by a modernist architecture
item.description: An impromptu tour of Mozambique's capital city reveals a unique imprint left by architect Amâncio "Pancho" Guedes.
item.content:encoded: An impromptu tour of Mozambique's capital city reveals a unique imprint left by architect Amâncio "Pancho" Guedes.
item.dc:creator: Matt Ozug

item.title: Taiwan says Chinese pressure over the island is the 'new normal'
item.description: Taiwan's Foreign Ministry on Tuesday said the scholars' passports and mobile phones were confiscated, and they were detained in Mombasa for more than 20 hours before being allowed to leave the country.
item.content:encoded:
Taiwan's Foreign Ministry on Tuesday said the scholars' passports and mobile phones were confiscated, and they were detained in Mombasa for more than 20 hours before being allowed to leave the country.

(Image credit: Brian Inganga)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump to face questions at G7 press conference. And, Tuesday's primary results
item.description: Trump has touted his tentative agreement with Iran at the G7 summit. Today, he is expected to field questions at a press conference as the summit wraps up. And, a look at Tuesday's primary election results.
item.content:encoded:
Trump has touted his tentative agreement with Iran at the G7 summit. Today, he is expected to field questions at a press conference as the summit wraps up. And, a look at Tuesday's primary election results.

(Image credit: Ludovix Marin)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Trump signs preliminary agreement with Iran, after releasing details
item.description: The Trump administration shared the details of the preliminary agreement to end the war with Iran on Wednesday. President Trump later said he had signed it, during a dinner with French President Emmanuel Macron.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration shared the details of the preliminary agreement to end the war with Iran on Wednesday. President Trump later said he had signed it, during a dinner with French President Emmanuel Macron.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: Trump expected to face questions about Iran war as he wraps up G7 summit
item.description: President Trump's G7 summit meetings with world leaders conclude in a news conference, with questions likely to address an uncertain agreement to end war with Iran and U.S. support for Ukraine.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's G7 summit meetings with world leaders conclude in a news conference, with questions likely to address an uncertain agreement to end war with Iran and U.S. support for Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez
